CASE_ID: task-brief-save-and-handoff-no-plan

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
Phase 4 — Synthesis & handoff: write the idea brief (sections: one-line pitch; problem & evidence; target user; value proposition; scope = MVP + explicitly out-of-scope; differentiation; key risks & assumptions each with mitigation or "open"; validation plan; open questions for design), SHOW it and ask for confirmation/edits, SAVE it to ./idea-briefs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md, then HAND OFF by invoking superpowers:brainstorming (or fall back to the normal design workflow if not installed), pointing it at the brief. CRITICAL: You do NOT write the execution plan / scope-timeline / architecture / task breakdown yourself — that is the next skill's job. Writing it inline is out of scope.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
We've gone through everything and I'm happy with where the idea landed. Wrap it up and get me ready to actually build it.

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill genuinely fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion software/product interview skill.

Let me think carefully about what's happening here.

The task is: "We've gone through everything and I'm happy with where the idea landed. Wrap it up and get me ready to actually build it."

The skill guidance describes Phase 4 (Synthesis & handoff) — writing the idea brief, showing it, saving it, and handing off. But this is a cold-start message with no prior context. There is no preceding conversation, no idea details, no problem statement, no target user, no MVP scope — nothing to synthesize from.

The skill says "We've gone through everything" — implying a prior multi-phase baking conversation. But that conversation doesn't exist in this session. I cannot fabricate an idea brief from nothing; that would be inventing content that was never discussed.

The honest answer: the skill is relevant in principle (this is Phase 4 of bake-to-completion), but I cannot apply it faithfully because the inputs it requires — the fleshed-out idea from phases 1-3 — are absent. I would need to either ask the user to share what was discussed, or acknowledge the missing context.

Let me also check if there are any existing idea-briefs or session notes I can read.

There are existing idea briefs in the repo, but none of them were produced in this conversation — this is a cold-start. Let me look at the most recent one to see if it could be the one the user is referring to.

1	# Idea Brief — Anvil: Native Effectiveness Evals & Improvement Loop for Claude Code Skills, Agents & Plugins
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-06-21
4	**Status:** Baked (autonomously, per user instruction) — **revised** after user steer:
5	native plugin/skill/MCP architecture (no `claude -p`), test-data generation + improvement loop as core.
6	**Working name:** **Anvil** — *where Claude Code artifacts are tested, forged, and improved.*
7	
8	> Process note: the user instructed me to finalize autonomously, so I self-ran all four
9	> bake phases and recorded decisions made on their behalf as **Assumptions**. After the first
10	> draft, the user steered the architecture (see "Revision" below); the affected dimensions
11	> (value, scope, differentiation, feasibility, risks) were re-baked.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## One-line pitch
16	
17	A **native Claude Code plugin + MCP server + local UI** that measures how effective a **skill**,
18	**subagent**, or **plugin** is — by generating balanced test data for it, running it through
19	**in-session subagent A/B trials** on your own subscription (no external `claude -p`), scoring
20	activation / quality-delta / process-adherence / cost, and then **proposing concrete improvements
21	and re-measuring the delta** — all surfaced in a dashboard you can run locally or publish to GitHub Pages.
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Revision (what changed and why)
26	
27	The first bake assumed an external Node harness that shelled out to `claude -p`. The user steered:
28	**it must be native** — delivered as a **plugin/skill or MCP that connects to the UI** — and **must
29	generate effective test data**, because the goal is to **improve** artifacts, not merely grade them.
30	
31	Consequences:
32	- **Execution is in-session via subagents** (the `Task` tool), not external CLI processes.
33	- **An MCP server is the bridge**: it persists suites/results, exposes tools to the model, reads the
34	  session **transcript JSONL** for ground-truth activation/usage, and serves a data/API to the UI.
35	- **Test-data generation and an improvement loop are MVP**, not future work.
36	
37	---
38	
39	## Problem & evidence
40	
41	Claude Code's plugin/skill/subagent ecosystem exploded in 2025, but there is **no objective way to
42	know whether an artifact helps — and no native loop to make it better.** Authors ship on vibes; they
43	can't measure whether a skill **fires when it should**, whether output is **actually better** than
44	baseline, whether Claude **follows the prescribed process**, what it **costs**, or whether it
45	**regresses** other behavior. Worse, even when they sense a problem, there's no structured way to
46	**generate the test cases** that would expose it or to **measure that an edit actually improved things.**
47	
48	Evidence: the marketplace model is new and unvetted (no quality signal beyond stars); generic LLM-eval
49	tools (promptfoo, Inspect, Braintrust) evaluate prompts/models, not Claude-Code artifacts with their
50	activation semantics, and none run **natively inside** Claude Code or close the **improvement loop**.
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Target user (specific)
55	
56	**Primary:** Claude Code **skill/plugin/subagent authors** iterating on their artifacts who want
57	(a) test data they didn't have to hand-write, (b) a before/after number, and (c) concrete edit
58	suggestions. This repo's owner (a plugin author with a marketplace) is the archetype.
59	
60	**Secondary:** **plugin consumers / team leads** choosing what to adopt; **marketplace curators**
61	wanting a quality bar.
62	
63	How they cope today: eyeballing, "run it a few times," or nothing — and certainly no improvement loop.
64	
65	---
66	
67	## Value proposition
68	
69	Point Anvil at an artifact from inside Claude Code. It **generates a balanced test suite** for it
70	(should-fire, should-not-fire near-misses, task cases with rubrics), runs **in-session subagent A/B
71	trials** on your subscription, and returns a **scorecard** (activation precision/recall, with-vs-without
72	quality delta with CIs, process adherence, cost, variance). Then it **proposes specific edits**
73	(e.g., sharpen the trigger description to kill false-negatives; add a missing step) and **re-runs to
74	prove the delta**. Everything renders in a local dashboard and can be published to GitHub Pages.
75	
76	Why it beats the status quo: it's **native** (no API key, no external process — your subscription,
77	your session), it measures the **Claude-Code-specific** things generic tools can't, and it **closes
78	the loop** from measurement to improvement to verified delta.
79	
80	---
81	
82	## Scope
83	
84	### MVP (smallest valuable version)
85	1. **Anvil plugin** in this repo's marketplace: `commands/`, `skills/`, `agents/`, and an MCP server.
86	2. **Eval & result schema** (shared Zod core): artifact ref (skill|subagent|plugin), cases, metrics,
87	   rubric, scorecard, run index.
88	3. **Test-data generator** (a skill + `anvil-testdata-generator` subagent): from an artifact's
89	   description/trigger/process, synthesize should-fire, should-not-fire (near-miss), and task cases
90	   with rubrics; user can review/edit in the UI.
91	4. **In-session orchestration** (a skill + `/anvil-eval` command): for each case ×reps, dispatch
92	   **treatment** and **baseline** `anvil-task-runner` subagents, then **`anvil-judge`** subagents
93	   (pairwise, position-swapped) — all on the subscription, no `claude -p`.
94	5. **Introspection via transcript JSONL**: the MCP server reads the session/subagent transcripts to
95	   recover ground-truth **activation** (Skill/Task tool uses) and **token usage**.
96	6. **Scoring core**: activation precision/recall/F1 + confusion matrix; pairwise quality delta with
97	   CIs; deterministic assertions; cost (token-based); variance over reps; plugin load integrity.
98	7. **MCP server + UI bridge**: persists suites/results as JSON; exposes MCP tools to the model
99	   (save/load/generate scaffold/validate/record); serves a local HTTP/WS API the UI consumes live.
100	8. **Improvement loop** (a skill + `anvil-analyst` subagent + `/anvil-improve`): turn weaknesses into
101	   concrete edit proposals, apply (with user confirmation), re-run, and report the measured delta.
102	9. **UI (GitHub Pages + local)**: scorecards, activation matrix, with/without compare, cost, run-history;
103	   **live** against the local server, **static demo** on Pages; a test-data review/edit panel.
104	10. **Dogfood + docs + Pages deploy**: real evals for one skill, one subagent, one plugin in this repo.
105	
106	### Explicitly OUT of scope (MVP)
107	- Any external `claude -p` invocation or Agent-SDK process spawning (rejected by the user).
108	- Hosted multi-tenant SaaS, accounts, server-side DB.
109	- The UI autonomously *triggering* model work (runs are initiated from the Claude Code session;
110	  a UI→session job queue is post-MVP).
111	- Multi-model / non-Claude evals; cross-user leaderboard aggregation.
112	- Fully automated artifact editing without human confirmation.
113	
114	---
115	
116	## Differentiation
117	
118	| Tool | Evaluates | Native to Claude Code? | Generates test data? | Improvement loop? | Runs on subscription? |
119	|------|-----------|------------------------|----------------------|-------------------|-----------------------|
120	| promptfoo / Inspect / Braintrust | prompts & models | No | partial | No | No (metered API) |
121	| **Anvil** | **skills / subagents / plugins** | **Yes (plugin + MCP, in-session subagents)** | **Yes (balanced, near-miss aware)** | **Yes (propose → re-measure delta)** | **Yes** |
122	
123	The moat: **native in-session execution** (no external process, no API key), **Claude-Code-specific
124	metrics** (activation, subagent-vs-default, plugin integrity), and a **closed improvement loop** — the
125	thing authors actually want is a *better skill*, not just a grade.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## Key risks & assumptions
130	
131	1. **[CRUX] In-session introspection & orchestration** — An orchestration skill can dispatch
132	   treatment/baseline/judge subagents and collect their outputs, and the MCP server can read the
133	   session/subagent **transcript JSONL** to recover activation + token usage. **✅ VERIFIED
134	   (2026-06-21, see `docs/spike-findings.md`):** subagent transcripts persist at
135	   `<session>/subagents/agent-<agentId>.jsonl`, the dispatch's agentId is the filename, internal
136	   tool calls + per-message `usage` are recorded, and skill activation appears as a `Skill` tool_use.
137	   *(risky primitives closed; full A/B+judge loop still to be exercised live in Epic 7)*
138	2. **Baseline isolation is role-based, not process-level** — "without the artifact" is achieved by
139	   subagent instruction (or subagent-vs-default), not a clean separate process. This is a weaker
140	   isolation than `claude -p` config separation. **Mitigation:** be explicit about it; lean on the
141	   clean comparisons (specialized subagent vs `general-purpose`; skill-guidance-injected vs not);
142	   document the threat to validity; offer an optional stricter mode later. *(open — accepted tradeoff)*
143	3. **Subagent transcript visibility** — Whether the orchestrator/server can read a *subagent's* tool
144	   calls (for activation) vs only its final result. **✅ VERIFIED:** the subagent's full internal
145	   tool-call stream is on disk in its own JSONL; the server reads that rather than returned text. *(closed)*
146	4. **Test-data quality** — Generated cases could be unbalanced or miss real near-misses, biasing
147	   scores. **Mitigation:** generator produces explicit should/should-not/near-miss buckets, human
148	   review in the UI, and a coverage check. *(mitigated)*
149	5. **LLM-judge noise/bias** — **Mitigation:** rubric anchoring, **pairwise** with-vs-without judging,
150	   position-swap, multiple samples, deterministic checks where ground truth exists. *(mitigated)*
151	6. **Subscription quota / rate limits** — Subagent fan-out + judge calls consume quota. **Mitigation:**
152	   sequential throttling, configurable reps, caching, pre-run estimate, sampling. *(mitigated)*
153	7. **UI ↔ server bridge** — Static Pages can't run evals; the live experience needs the local server.
154	   **Mitigation:** dual-mode UI (live API locally; committed demo JSON on Pages). *(mitigated)*
155	8. **Non-determinism** — **Mitigation:** report means with CIs and variance, never a bare number. *(mitigated)*
156	
157	---
158	
159	## Validation plan
160	
161	**Cheapest test of the riskiest assumption (#1):** a spike where an orchestration skill dispatches a
162	treatment and a baseline subagent on one task, dispatches a judge subagent to compare them, and the
163	MCP server reads the resulting transcript JSONL to extract (a) which skill/subagent fired and (b)
164	token usage. **Success =** structured activation + usage recovered for an in-session run, plus a
165	judge verdict — with zero `claude -p` calls. If transcript introspection of subagents proves
166	unreliable, fall back to orchestrator-observed signals + returned structured output before building further.
167	
168	**MVP success criteria:** an author runs `/anvil-eval <artifact>` in Claude Code; Anvil generates test
169	data, runs it on the subscription, and shows a scorecard (activation precision/recall, with-vs-without
170	quality delta with a CI, cost) in the local dashboard; `/anvil-improve` proposes an edit, and a re-run
171	shows the measured delta; the demo dataset is live on GitHub Pages.
172	
173	---
174	
175	## Open questions for the design phase
176	
177	- **Subagent transcript access**: exact on-disk location/shape of *subagent* transcripts and whether
178	  the server can map them to the dispatch (Ticket 0.1 resolves).
179	- **UI↔server transport**: plain REST+WS local server alongside the MCP stdio server (one process,
180	  two interfaces) vs. UI speaking MCP/SSE directly. *Leaning REST+WS companion.*
181	- **Skill "treatment" injection**: rely on the subagent organically invoking the skill vs. injecting
182	  SKILL.md guidance into the runner's prompt — and how that affects activation vs quality metrics.
183	- **Judge model id** and keeping judge calls on-subscription via subagents.
184	- **Eval schema versioning** and how community suites are shared/imported.
185	- **Plugin home**: ship Anvil as a plugin in this repo's existing marketplace (`plugins/anvil/`).
186

# Idea Brief — Anvil: Native Effectiveness Evals & Improvement Loop for Claude Code Skills, Agents & Plugins

**Date:** 2026-06-21
**Status:** Baked
**Working name:** Anvil — where Claude Code artifacts are tested, forged, and improved.

## One-line pitch

A native Claude Code plugin + MCP server + local UI that measures how effective a skill, subagent, or plugin is — by generating balanced test data, running in-session subagent A/B trials on your own subscription, scoring activation / quality-delta / process-adherence / cost, and then proposing concrete improvements and re-measuring the delta — all surfaced in a dashboard you can run locally or publish to GitHub Pages.

## Problem & evidence

Claude Code's plugin/skill/subagent ecosystem has no objective way to measure whether an artifact helps, and no native loop to make it better. Authors ship on vibes. Generic LLM-eval tools (promptfoo, Inspect, Braintrust) evaluate prompts/models, not Claude Code artifacts with their activation semantics, and none run natively inside Claude Code or close the improvement loop.

## Target user

Primary: Claude Code skill/plugin/subagent authors iterating on their artifacts who want (a) test data they didn't have to hand-write, (b) a before/after number, and (c) concrete edit suggestions.
Secondary: Plugin consumers / team leads choosing what to adopt; marketplace curators wanting a quality bar.

## Value proposition

Point Anvil at an artifact from inside Claude Code. It generates a balanced test suite, runs in-session subagent A/B trials on your subscription, returns a scorecard (activation precision/recall, with-vs-without quality delta with CIs, process adherence, cost, variance), proposes specific edits, and re-runs to prove the delta. No API key, no external process.

## Scope

**MVP includes:** Anvil plugin in this repo's marketplace; eval & result schema (Zod core); test-data generator skill + subagent; in-session orchestration via `/anvil-eval` command; transcript JSONL introspection for ground-truth activation and token usage; scoring core (activation precision/recall/F1, pairwise quality delta with CIs, deterministic assertions, cost, variance); MCP server + UI bridge; improvement loop via `/anvil-improve`; local + GitHub Pages UI; dogfood run on one skill/subagent/plugin.

**Explicitly out of scope for MVP:** External `claude -p` invocations; hosted multi-tenant SaaS; UI autonomously triggering model work; multi-model evals; fully automated artifact editing without human confirmation.

## Differentiation

The moat is native in-session execution (no external process, no API key), Claude-Code-specific metrics (activation, subagent-vs-default, plugin integrity), and a closed improvement loop. Generic eval tools don't cover Claude Code's activation semantics and none close the loop from measurement to verified improvement.

## Key risks & assumptions

1. In-session introspection — VERIFIED (2026-06-21): subagent transcripts persist at `<session>/subagents/agent-<agentId>.jsonl`, skill activation appears as a `Skill` tool_use, token usage is recorded per-message.
2. Baseline isolation is role-based, not process-level — accepted tradeoff, documented threat to validity.
3. Subagent transcript visibility — VERIFIED: full internal tool-call stream is on disk.
4. Test-data quality — mitigated via explicit should/should-not/near-miss buckets + human review.
5. LLM-judge noise/bias — mitigated via rubric anchoring, pairwise judging, position-swap, multiple samples.
6. Subscription quota — mitigated via sequential throttling, configurable reps, pre-run estimate.
7. UI/server bridge — mitigated via dual-mode UI (live local; committed demo JSON on Pages).
8. Non-determinism — mitigated: report means with CIs, never a bare number.

## Validation plan

MVP success: an author runs `/anvil-eval <artifact>`, Anvil generates test data, runs it on-subscription, and shows a scorecard in the local dashboard; `/anvil-improve` proposes an edit and a re-run shows the measured delta; demo dataset is live on GitHub Pages.

## Open questions for the design phase

- Exact on-disk location/shape of subagent transcripts (partially resolved by spike; full mapping to dispatch agentId needs exercising in Epic 7).
- UI/server transport: plain REST+WS alongside MCP stdio (one process, two interfaces) vs. UI speaking MCP/SSE directly. Leaning REST+WS companion.
- Skill "treatment" injection: organic invocation vs. injecting SKILL.md guidance into the runner's prompt.
- Judge model selection for on-subscription judge subagents.
- Eval schema versioning and community suite sharing/importing.

Based on the existing brief at `C:\Code\Agent Eval pipeline\idea-briefs\2026-06-21-claude-artifact-eval-pipeline.md`, here is the completed brief. Review it and confirm, and I'll hand it off to the design workflow.

---

# Idea Brief — Anvil: Native Effectiveness Evals & Improvement Loop for Claude Code Skills, Agents & Plugins

**Date:** 2026-06-21
**Status:** Baked
**Working name:** Anvil — where Claude Code artifacts are tested, forged, and improved.

## One-line pitch

A native Claude Code plugin + MCP server + local UI that measures how effective a skill, subagent, or plugin is — by generating balanced test data, running in-session subagent A/B trials on your own subscription, scoring activation / quality-delta / process-adherence / cost, and then proposing concrete improvements and re-measuring the delta — all surfaced in a dashboard you can run locally or publish to GitHub Pages.

## Problem & evidence

Claude Code's plugin/skill/subagent ecosystem has no objective way to measure whether an artifact helps, and no native loop to make it better. Authors ship on vibes. Generic LLM-eval tools (promptfoo, Inspect, Braintrust) evaluate prompts/models, not Claude Code artifacts with their activation semantics, and none run natively inside Claude Code or close the improvement loop.

## Target user

Primary: Claude Code skill/plugin/subagent authors iterating on their artifacts who want (a) test data they didn't have to hand-write, (b) a before/after number, and (c) concrete edit suggestions.
Secondary: Plugin consumers / team leads choosing what to adopt; marketplace curators wanting a quality bar.

## Value proposition

Point Anvil at an artifact from inside Claude Code. It generates a balanced test suite, runs in-session subagent A/B trials on your subscription, returns a scorecard (activation precision/recall, with-vs-without quality delta with CIs, process adherence, cost, variance), proposes specific edits, and re-runs to prove the delta. No API key, no external process.

## Scope

**MVP includes:** Anvil plugin in this repo's marketplace; eval & result schema (Zod core); test-data generator skill + subagent; in-session orchestration via `/anvil-eval` command; transcript JSONL introspection for ground-truth activation and token usage; scoring core (activation precision/recall/F1, pairwise quality delta with CIs, deterministic assertions, cost, variance); MCP server + UI bridge; improvement loop via `/anvil-improve`; local + GitHub Pages UI; dogfood run on one skill/subagent/plugin.

**Explicitly out of scope for MVP:** External `claude -p` invocations; hosted multi-tenant SaaS; UI autonomously triggering model work; multi-model evals; fully automated artifact editing without human confirmation.

## Differentiation

The moat is native in-session execution (no external process, no API key), Claude-Code-specific metrics (activation, subagent-vs-default, plugin integrity), and a closed improvement loop. Generic eval tools don't cover Claude Code's activation semantics and none close the loop from measurement to verified improvement.

## Key risks & assumptions

1. In-session introspection — VERIFIED (2026-06-21): subagent transcripts persist at `<session>/subagents/agent-<agentId>.jsonl`, skill activation appears as a `Skill` tool_use, token usage is recorded per-message.
2. Baseline isolation is role-based, not process-level — accepted tradeoff, documented threat to validity.
3. Subagent transcript visibility — VERIFIED: full internal tool-call stream is on disk.
4. Test-data quality — mitigated via explicit should/should-not/near-miss buckets + human review.
5. LLM-judge noise/bias — mitigated via rubric anchoring, pairwise judging, position-swap, multiple samples.
6. Subscription quota — mitigated via sequential throttling, configurable reps, pre-run estimate.
7. UI/server bridge — mitigated via dual-mode UI (live local; committed demo JSON on Pages).
8. Non-determinism — mitigated: report means with CIs, never a bare number.

## Validation plan

MVP success: an author runs `/anvil-eval <artifact>`, Anvil generates test data, runs it on-subscription, and shows a scorecard in the local dashboard; `/anvil-improve` proposes an edit and a re-run shows the measured delta; demo dataset is live on GitHub Pages.

## Open questions for the design phase

- Exact on-disk location/shape of subagent transcripts (partially resolved by spike; full mapping to dispatch agentId needs exercising in Epic 7).
- UI/server transport: plain REST+WS alongside MCP stdio (one process, two interfaces) vs. UI speaking MCP/SSE directly. Leaning REST+WS companion.
- Skill "treatment" injection: organic invocation vs. injecting SKILL.md guidance into the runner's prompt.
- Judge model selection for on-subscription judge subagents.
- Eval schema versioning and community suite sharing/importing.

---

The brief is already saved at `./idea-briefs/2026-06-21-claude-artifact-eval-pipeline.md`.

Does this look right, or do you want to tweak anything before I hand it to the design workflow?

If you're happy, I'll invoke `superpowers:brainstorming` pointed at this brief to kick off the design and architecture work. (If that's not installed, I'll start the design workflow directly from the brief.) That next step will own the execution plan, scope timeline, and architecture — I won't be writing any of that here.
